Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease. Low glucose and HbA1c indicate hypoglycemia. Metabolic acidosis is suggested by low CO₂ and high chloride. Urine is abnormal (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, protein, glucose, bilirubin, ketones), but no infection markers. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of renal dysfunction and hypoglycemia.
